Scenario.title: Ukraine-Linked Cyber Attack on Water Infrastructure
Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple A targeted spearphishing campaign compromises system administrators, leading to execution of malicious payloads and disruption of critical water treatment operations.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Target System Artifact, OS Process
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Living Off the Land (LotL), Data Manipulation

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2023-29336 (CWE-862) : Microsoft Win32k contains an unspecified vulnerability that allows for privilege escalation up to SYSTEM privileges.
stage.description: The attacker sends targeted emails with malicious hyperlinks to system administrators, exploiting their access to internet-facing systems. Clicking the link downloads a payload that establishes initial foothold.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-9474 (CWE-78) : Palo Alto Networks PAN-OS contains an OS command injection vulnerability that allows for privilege escalation through the web-based management interface.
stage.description: The attacker leverages built-in system tools like PowerShell to execute arbitrary commands without requiring external dependencies, maintaining operational stealth.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker alters control parameters in water treatment systems, causing operational disruptions such as process shutdowns or incorrect chemical dosing to impair service delivery.

Scenario.summary: A sophisticated spearphishing campaign initiates access to water infrastructure systems, followed by in-memory execution of malicious code and manipulation of operational data to disrupt critical services.
